scope: mvp-build non-mechanical planning and implementation decisions

proof_classes.P0_representation_calculation: computed score vector embedding mode clustering centrality simulation or other observation about the chosen representation
proof_classes.P1_formal_model_property: theorem machine-checkable certificate verified eigenstructure solver result model-checker result or invariant proof for an explicitly declared model and assumptions
proof_classes.P2_representation_fidelity: proof that the formal or learned representation corresponds to the source runtime or evidence boundary it claims to model
proof_classes.P3_executable_software: accepted exact-candidate source type unit integration database browser build image or related executable proof
proof_classes.P4_external_production_acceptance: managed provider target-host real-channel accessibility commercial recovery trusted-signing pilot deployment or production evidence
proof_classes.correspondence_rule: P1 is proof for its declared formal property; P1 plus sufficient P2 may satisfy a P3 gate only when that gate defines and verifies the correspondence; no proof class is demoted or silently expanded

Adj.meaning: useful adjacency to the current boundary

ProofDensity.meaning: independent proof obligations addressed per unit scope across declared proof classes

trace007.schema_rule: every score dimension is classified exactly once; required dimensions must exist; optional absence is reported; unclassified dimensions fail closed

allowed_metrics: spectral and geometric certificates for declared graph properties
candidate_graph.forbidden_claim: software invariant completion without a declared mapping and proof contract

software_invariant_hypergraph.vertices: actual software entities states or obligations
software_invariant_hypergraph.proved_rule: the hyperedge declared proof contract is satisfied; P1 formal proof is sufficient for a formal-model edge, while executable-software edges additionally require the declared P2/P3 correspondence

spectral.proof_status: P1 when theorem assumptions operator semantics residuals conditioning and reproducibility are satisfied
spectral.guard: a dominant eigenvector may be decisive for the declared mathematical problem; mapping it to a software decision requires explicit fidelity and objective correspondence

feasible_domain.rule: All objective variants and baselines use the same mandatory-coverage prerequisite and invariant-feasible domain.
feasible_domain.formal_membership: a P1 plus sufficient P2 certificate may prove feasible-domain membership

baseline_rules.membership: declared explicitly; never inferred from names aliases weight signs or whatever dimensions happen to exist
baseline_rules.schema_change: a newly introduced unclassified score dimension fails with unclassified_baseline_dimensions
baseline_rules.grouping: semantic group weights prevent schema density from changing baseline importance

causality.selection_influencing: the term changes a selected candidate set inside the same feasible domain
causality.formal_proof: a valid P1 P2 or P3 certificate proves the exact property declared by its proof class
causality.causal_improvement: an appropriate formal causal argument or equal-feasibility implementation ablation improves independent outcomes such as defects found complete proof obligations coherence review defects unnecessary scope or executable verification yield

sensitivity.search: report unique optima objective spread and selected-set stability across deterministic restarts
sensitivity.weights: report selected-set software-coverage and certificate stability under declared bounded perturbations
sensitivity.spectral: report operator construction normalization residual multiplicity conditioning and sensitivity to plausible edge and weight changes
sensitivity.failure_rule: instability is reported and reduces the scope or confidence of the claim; it is not hidden by choosing a favorable run

mathematics_guards.hypergraph: separate candidate topology from software invariant topology and preserve genuine many-way relations
mathematics_guards.spectral: eigenvalues eigenvectors eigenspaces singular vectors and projectors may be P1 proof when operator semantics theorem assumptions residuals conditioning and reproducibility are valid
mathematics_guards.hodge: use only for a true simplicial or cellular complex with valid boundary operators
mathematics_guards.koopman: use with repeated comparable trajectories fitted propagation held-out evaluation and diversity or residual control; algebraic fitted-model properties may be P1 while predictive claims require held-out evidence
mathematics_guards.predictive_models: disabled for predictive ranking when they do not outperform a simpler evidence-and-invariants baseline on held-out outcomes
mathematics_guards.continuous_thought: COCONUT latent BFS hidden-state recursion manifold or continuous-thought language may inspire representation design but retained claims require inspectable artifacts and verification
mathematics_guards.unknown: never coerce to observed zero risk or positive evidence
mathematics_guards.anti_rationalization: do not construct topology operators weights or embeddings after selecting the desired patch merely to justify it
